heading: Clothing for destitute shipwrecked individuals
label: 2777 Clothing for destitute shipwrecked individuals
text: The Coast Guard may furnish clothing and subsistence to destitute shipwrecked individuals, and the Coast Guard may reimburse, in cash or in kind, Coast Guard personnel who furnish clothing and subsistence to destitute shipwrecked individuals.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 33, U.S.C., 1946 ed., § 749 (, ; , ).July 27, 1912, ch. 255, § 237 Stat. 239Mar. 4, 1913, ch. 141, § 137 Stat. 736This section was enlarged to provide that the Coast Guard, as well as personnel thereof, may furnish clothing and subsistence to destitute shipwrecked persons.Changes were made in phraseology. 81st Congress, House Report No. 557.
Amendments2021— substituted “individuals” for “persons” in section catchline and in two places in text.Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282section 486 of this title
